text:
Once upon a time in a small, quiet town, there was a kind-hearted librarian named Martha. Martha loved books more than anything else in the world—each one an old friend, each shelf a treasure chest. She spent hours arranging them by genre, color, and size, her fingers tracing the spines like a gentle caress.

Martha had a special shelf just for fairy tales. It was lined with velvet, and its light catched only the faintest dapples from the slivered sunlight seeping through the old bay windows. Her collection was rich with tales of courage, princes and princesses, and magical lands. Each story was a gateway to a different kind of adventure, and Martha felt a special connection to these stories.

One brisk winter day, Martha found herself at her wit’s end. The library had fallen silent—all of her usual visitors had retreated inside to keep warm. As a storm raged outside, her imagination ran wild, and in a rush of whims
